text.title: Puppeteer Agent Workflow Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the puppeteer-agent-workflow methodology. Each pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: The agent MUST drive Puppeteer through a separate worker process invoked via Bash with a hard --timeout. Running Puppeteer inline inside the LLM turn is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Browser processes are stateful and long-lived; the LLM is stateless and token-bounded. Inline drives produce unbounded waits, memory leaks, and unrecoverable state on context compaction.

rule.statement: The worker MUST write structured artifacts (JSON, screenshots, HAR) to disk and exit. The agent reads only the artifact path back, never streams live page state into context.
rule.rationale: Streaming raw page.content() into the LLM blows the context budget and hallucinates layout. Structured artifacts are bounded and re-readable across compactions.

rule.statement: Selectors MUST be attribute-based (data-testid, id, name, role). Selectors derived from a screenshot (nth-child, visual class names) are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Positional selectors encode pixel coordinates or visual class names that break on any CSS/layout change; attribute-based selectors survive redesigns.

rule.statement: Before persisting any artifact (HAR, log, screenshot, JSON), the worker MUST run a secret scrubber that masks tokens, cookies, passwords, and PII patterns.
rule.rationale: Automated pipelines logging full page state will leak session tokens; raw HAR contains Authorization headers verbatim.

rule.statement: Flows hitting login forms, CAPTCHA challenges, or 2FA prompts MUST pause and surface a screenshot for human review. Silent retry loops on auth walls are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Automated retries against credential walls trigger account lockout and never succeed; a screenshot + pause unblocks the human and avoids the lockout.

rule.statement: The worker MUST be invoked with a wall-clock timeout (e.g., timeout 60 node worker.js). The script itself MUST set page.setDefaultTimeout to a value below the wall-clock budget.
rule.rationale: Without timeouts, a hanging waitFor* call burns the agent's quota until the orchestrator kills it. A nested timeout keeps the failure mode explicit.
